How was the voice of the Lord heard by Elijah the Tishbite? It was not the "strong wind [which] rent the mountains, and brake in pieces the rocks," nor "after the wind an earthquake," nor "after the earthquake a fire." It was "a still small voice" (see 1 Kgs. 19:11-12 [1 Kings 19:11-12]). It is the inner voice of the Spirit, which has the capacity to whisper through and pierce all things (see D&C 85:6). As well as wearing a girdle of skin about his loins, Elijah wore a mantle, a very common article of clothing in that day. Generally it was simply a sheepskin. 18 Yet I have left me seven thousand in Israel, all the knees which have not bowed unto Baal, and every mouth which hath not kissed him.

In Elijah's interview he was told that he was not alone, that the Lord had left "seven thousand in Israel, all the knees which have not bowed unto Baal, and every mouth which hath not kissed him." (1 Kings 19:18.) Elijah also was instructed to anoint a new king over Syria and a new king over Israel. He was told who they were to be, and then he was told to find Elisha, the son of Shaphat, to be the prophet to succeed him. (Boyd K. Packer, The Holy Temple, p. 104-107)

(1 Kings 19:18.) Elijah also was instructed to anoint a new king over Syria and a new king over Israel. He was told who they were to be, and then he was told to find Elisha, the son of Shaphat, to be the prophet to succeed him. (Boyd K. Packer, The Holy Temple, p. 104-107)
